question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordItem with a manipulated getTransactionFee() value in the TransactionRecord protobuf such that chargedTxFee is persisted with an attacker-controlled amount, bypassing any fee validation in the parser, causing incorrect fee data to be stored and surfaced via the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that results in a RecordItem whose getConsensusTimestamp() falls exactly on the boundary of dateRangeFilter.filter(), causing the item to be processed by aggregator.accept() (accumulating gasUsed) but skipped by recordItemListener.onItem(), leading to a mismatch between persisted fee records and the aggregated gasUsed on the RecordFile?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a contract call transaction that inflates the getGasUsed() value in the ContractFunctionResult protobuf, causing gasUsed to accumulate unboundedly in the RecordItemAggregator without overflow protection, resulting in a negative or wrapped gasUsed value being persisted to the record_file table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.update()] Can an unprivileged user cause a RecordFile to be processed with an artificially large gasUsed value by submitting many contract transactions with maximum gas values, such that recordFile.setGasUsed(gasUsed) stores a value exceeding design parameters, causing downstream fee calculations based on gasUsed to be incorrect?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the @Retryable annotation on parse() to cause a RecordFile to be re-processed multiple times after a transient failure, resulting in duplicate fee records being persisted or gasUsed being double-counted in the RecordItemAggregator?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where the @Transactional boundary on parse() rolls back after partial fee processing, but the RecordItemAggregator's gasUsed state is not reset, causing a subsequent retry to accumulate gasUsed from both the failed and retried run, persisting an inflated fee total?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a zero or negative getTransactionFee() in the TransactionRecord, which is accepted without validation by doParse() and passed directly to recordItemListener.onItem(), causing chargedTxFee to be stored as zero or negative, misrepresenting the actual fee charged?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordItem where getTransactionType() returns an unknown type not present in the latencyMetrics or sizeMetrics maps, causing recordMetrics() to fall back to unknownSizeMetric and unknownLatencyMetric, thereby masking fee anomalies from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordMetrics()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a serialized size of zero or Long.MAX_VALUE, causing sizeMetrics.getOrDefault().record() to record an out-of-range value that corrupts the hiero.mirror.importer.transaction.size metric, masking fee-related anomaly detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a version transition from v5 to v6 record files with a crafted index gap, causing updateIndex() to call repository.updateIndex(offset) with a large negative offset, shifting all block indices and corrupting the block-to-fee mapping used by downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged user cause a RecordFile with a manipulated getIndex() value to be processed, such that the computed offset in updateIndex() (recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1) is extremely large, triggering a bulk UPDATE on all record_file rows that shifts every block's index, breaking fee-to-block associations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user submit a RecordItem with a consensusTimestamp of Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE, causing dateRangeFilter.filter() to behave unexpectedly and either always include or always exclude the item, leading to fee records being silently dropped or incorrectly persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user cause a RecordFile with an empty items list to be parsed, such that aggregator.update() sets gasUsed to 0 and logsBloom to empty on the RecordFile, overwriting previously correct values if the file is re-processed, causing fee data loss?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction where isTopLevel() returns false (e.g., by setting a parentConsensusTimestamp), causing the item to be skipped by the aggregator but still processed by recordItemListener.onItem(), resulting in gasUsed being under-counted relative to the fees actually charged?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordItem where both hasContractCreateResult() and hasContractCallResult() return false, causing the aggregator to skip gasUsed accumulation for a transaction that actually consumed gas, leading to under-reported gasUsed on the RecordFile and incorrect fee accounting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user submit a RecordItem where getConsensusTimestamp() is identical to a previously processed item, causing logIndex assignment via setLogIndex(logIndex) to produce duplicate log indices, corrupting the ordering of fee-related contract events?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged user trigger processing of a large list of RecordFiles via parse(List<RecordFile>) where the @Transactional timeout is exceeded, causing a partial commit of fee records from some files but not others, leaving the database in an inconsistent fee state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user cause a RecordFile to contain a RecordItem with a getTransactionFee() value that exceeds the declared maxFee (body.getTransactionFee()), and since doParse() performs no validation of this invariant, the inflated chargedTxFee is persisted without any alert, misrepresenting the fee charged to the payer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that generates an AssessedCustomFee with an amount field set to Long.MAX_VALUE, which is accepted without bounds checking in insertAssessedCustomFees() called via recordItemListener.onItem(), causing overflow when fee amounts are summed in downstream REST API responses?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user craft a RecordItem with a negative getTransactionFee() in the TransactionRecord, which passes through doParse() without validation and is stored as a negative chargedTxFee, causing the REST API to report a negative fee for the transaction, misleading users about actual costs?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction with a ContractFunctionResult where getGasUsed() returns Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the gasUsed += result.getGasUsed() accumulation to overflow to a negative value, which is then persisted via recordFile.setGasUsed(gasUsed), corrupting gas fee accounting for the entire block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user cause a RecordFile to be processed where the dateRangeFilter excludes all items (count remains 0), but aggregator.update() still sets gasUsed and logsBloom from the aggregated (but unfiltered) items, creating a discrepancy between the count field and the gasUsed field on the persisted RecordFile?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that aggregator.accept() is called for ALL items regardless of dateRangeFilter, while recordItemListener.onItem() is only called for filtered items, to cause gasUsed to reflect fees from transactions that were not actually persisted, creating an inflated gasUsed on the RecordFile?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where parserContext.clear() in the finally block of parse() discards fee-related context that was partially committed, causing subsequent parse operations to operate on stale or missing fee state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged user submit a RecordItem with a getHighVolumePricingMultiplier() value of zero or a very large number in the TransactionRecord, which is stored without validation via transaction.setHighVolumePricingMultiplier(), causing fee multiplier data to be corrupted in the database?
